content.problemStatement:
Now we will add upvote and downvote functionality to comments. Allow logged-in users to cast, change, or retract a vote on any comment.

After this task:
- Upvote and downvote buttons (e.g., ⬆️ and ⬇️) will appear on each comment.
- When a logged-in user clicks a vote button, their vote is recorded.
- The comment's vote count (e.g., "1/0" for one upvote, zero downvotes) will update on the page.
- If a user clicks the same vote button again, their vote is removed.
- If they click the other vote button, their vote is changed.
- Logged-out users will see the vote counts but not the buttons.
### Files to Create/Modify for this Task:
- comments/models.py 
   - Add properties to the Comment model to easily calculate its score. 
   - Create a new Vote model to store each individual vote, linking a User to a Comment.
- comments/views.py 
   - Add the new vote_comment view. 
   - This view will contain all the logic for creating, updating, or deleting a Vote object.
- comments/urls.py 
   - Add a URL pattern that captures the comment ID and routes it to the new vote_comment view.
- comments/templates/comments/comment_display.html **(Done for you)**
   - Update the comment display to include the voting section. 
   - This involves adding two small forms (one for upvoting, one for downvoting) and displaying the upvote_count and downvote_count.

**After completing the above**:
- Click on **RUN** and open the browser on a new tab.
- Log in and go to a page with comments.
- Click the upvote button on a comment. The page should reload, and the score should change.
- Click the same upvote button again. The page should reload, and your vote should be removed.
- Click the downvote button. The page should reload, and your vote should be changed from an upvote to a downvote.
